own_review_points: Inconsistent Closeable vs AutoCloseable: Producer/QueueConsumer extend AutoCloseable; StreamConsumer/CheckpointConsumer extend Closeable (verified)
async receiveMulti returns CompletableFuture<List<Message>> losing Messages.lastId() (verified both async consumers)
receiverQueueSize on QueueConsumerBuilder but absent from StreamConsumerBuilder (verified)
ChunkingPolicy record lacks the static factory methods other config records provide (verified no static)
PulsarClientException nested static final classes lack serialVersionUID / @SuppressWarnings(serial) (verified)
build.gradle.kts adds compileOnly protobuf/opentelemetry deps unlike v4 pure-API module (verified)
notes: Human = LGTM minor, so the depth baseline is my own. This is a pure interface-definition PR; API-consistency findings ARE the substance. Verified against the visible diff: P1 Closeable/AutoCloseable (good), P3 async-List-loses-lastId (good), P5 receiverQueueSize gap (good), P6 ChunkingPolicy factories (good), P7 param-name (good), P8 serialVersionUID (good), P9 compileOnly deps (good). TWO factual errors: P2 claims QueueConsumer is 'missing consumerName()' -- it explicitly declares 'String consumerName();' (false 'X is missing' = wrong + fab). P4 is actually correct: AsyncQueueConsumer has acknowledge(Message<T>)/negativeAcknowledge(Message<T>) overloads that sync QueueConsumer lacks (verified) = good. P10 PulsarClientProvider suggestion = trivial. Cited files are all within the visible window (truncation at end), so no unverifiable. Net strong despite the one false missing-method claim.
